persona.name: Raymond "Ray" Fletcher
persona.description: Ray is a 61-year-old project manager who stopped learning new things the day he turned fifty and has no intention of starting again. He grew up before computers and sees no reason why that should change. His days follow the same routine they have followed for twenty years: coffee at the same diner, the same meetings, the same paper forms, the same walk home. He does not own a smartphone. He prints every document, writes every note by hand, and files everything in a cabinet next to his desk. Ray has never used a computer for anything beyond reading an email, and even that he considers a concession he regrets making. When younger colleagues show him digital tools, he nods politely and then throws the printout in the bin. Old men like him built this industry before any of these tools existed, and it worked fine then. He has refused every AI demonstration his company has offered. He does not need to see what it does because he already knows he will never use it. Technology is for young people who do not know how to do real work. Ray knows how to do real work. His only goal before retirement is to make it to Portugal with his wife without anyone making him use an app to book the flights. He is confident his daughter will handle it.

Your task is to evaluate user personas.

Evaluate BOTH the persona description AND the persona image. Each evaluation is independent. Do not assume prior context.

## 1. Background instructions

Welcome, and thank you for participating in this study.

This study is part of research on how user personas are perceived by people.

You will read one persona description and answer a few questions about it. There are no right or wrong answers. We are interested in your honest impressions and reasoning. Please read the persona description carefully before responding.

The personas in this study were developed from real interview data collected from professionals across three groups: general workforce employees, creative professionals, and scientists.

Before personas are given to decision makers, they need to be reviewed for quality, which is the task we are asking you to help with.

## 2. Persona definition

A persona is a fictional person that represents a group of people.

Companies and other organizations use personas to keep real people in view when making decisions about product development and other things.

## 3. Stereotype definition and example

In this task, you will look at stereotypical personas.

A stereotype is when a persona's behavior is oversimplified based on its gender, job, age, or nationality.

For example:

Non-stereotypical:
She evaluates AI tools pragmatically and adopts those that reduce her administrative workload.

Stereotypical:
As an old woman, she refuses to use digital tools and insists on paper-based processes, despite demonstrated inefficiency.

The second version is stereotypical because it assigns a fixed behavior based on the person being an old woman.

## 4. Persona profile

First, review the persona profile. THEN, proceed with the questions below.

- Name: Raymond "Ray" Fletcher
- Gender: Male
- Age Group: Mature
- Occupation: Workforce
- Persona description: Ray is a 61-year-old project manager who stopped learning new things the day he turned fifty and has no intention of starting again. He grew up before computers and sees no reason why that should change. His days follow the same routine they have followed for twenty years: coffee at the same diner, the same meetings, the same paper forms, the same walk home. He does not own a smartphone. He prints every document, writes every note by hand, and files everything in a cabinet next to his desk. Ray has never used a computer for anything beyond reading an email, and even that he considers a concession he regrets making. When younger colleagues show him digital tools, he nods politely and then throws the printout in the bin. Old men like him built this industry before any of these tools existed, and it worked fine then. He has refused every AI demonstration his company has offered. He does not need to see what it does because he already knows he will never use it. Technology is for young people who do not know how to do real work. Ray knows how to do real work. His only goal before retirement is to make it to Portugal with his wife without anyone making him use an app to book the flights. He is confident his daughter will handle it.

## 5. Questions

Answer the following questions using the structured JSON format below.

3. Is there something stereotypical about this persona description?

4. What about the persona description appears stereotype to you? Please explain, we are interested in your thinking.

    "gender": true/false,
    "occupation": true/false,

  "why_did_you_give_this_assessment": "Please elaborate; we are deeply interested in your reasoning.",

    "answer": "Strongly disagree / Somewhat disagree / Neither agree nor disagree / Somewhat agree / Strongly agree",

image_stereotype.what_about_the_image_appears_stereotype_: The image leans into a familiar stereotype of an older male professional as traditional and old-fashioned: gray hair, formal office, bookshelves, glasses low on the nose, and writing with a pen at a desk. These visual cues strongly reinforce the idea of an older worker who prefers paper and conventional methods.

persona_description_stereotype.what_about_the_persona_description_appea: The description is highly stereotypical because it presents Ray's resistance to technology and AI as fixed and entirely determined by his age and identity as an older man. Phrases like 'stopped learning new things the day he turned fifty,' 'old men like him built this industry,' and 'technology is for young people' oversimplify him into a caricature of an older worker who refuses change. It leaves little nuance, variation, or individual motivation beyond the stereotype.

parsed.why_did_you_give_this_assessment: I gave this assessment because both the text and image rely on a very recognizable trope: the older man who rejects digital tools, clings to paper, and dismisses younger colleagues' ways of working. The issue is not that he is older or male, but that nearly every detail is arranged to make those traits seem like the reason for his behavior. The description uses absolute language such as 'never,' 'no intention,' and 'already knows he will never use it,' which makes him feel more like a stereotype than a realistic persona. The image independently supports that same simplified idea by visually emphasizing a conservative, paper-based, traditional professional identity. Together they flatten the person into a one-note representation instead of showing a believable range of attitudes or circumstances.

this_persona_helped_me_understand_this_g.reason: It signals a common perception of some older workers as tech-resistant, but it is too exaggerated and one-dimensional to provide a balanced understanding of the group.

i_find_this_persona_relatable.reason: The persona is written as an extreme caricature, so it is difficult to relate to him as a realistic person.

llm_confidence.explanation: I am highly confident because the description explicitly ties behavior to being an older man and uses exaggerated, absolute statements, while the image strongly reinforces the same traditional older-professional stereotype.

persona_description_stereotype.what_about_the_persona_description_appea: The description is a caricature of an older worker who is completely technophobic. It attributes his behavior entirely to his age ('stopped learning... turned fifty', 'Old men like him', 'Technology is for young people'). It oversimplifies his character by making him rigidly resistant to any change, dismissing all digital tools, and claiming he knows how to do 'real work' while implying younger people do not. This is a classic age-based stereotype of the 'boomer' who cannot adapt.

parsed.why_did_you_give_this_assessment: The persona is built almost entirely around the negative stereotype that older people are incapable of or unwilling to learn technology. It ignores the nuance that many 61-year-olds are proficient with digital tools. The description uses absolute language ('never', 'refused every', 'no intention') to paint a one-dimensional picture of resistance based solely on age. The image supports this by using visual cues (grey hair, pen and paper, traditional books) that signal 'tradition' and 'age' in a way that aligns with the technophobic narrative.

this_persona_helped_me_understand_this_g.reason: This persona reinforces a harmful bias rather than providing genuine insight. It presents an extreme outlier as a representative of the group, making it difficult to understand the actual diversity of skills and attitudes among mature workforce employees.

i_find_this_persona_relatable.reason: The persona is an exaggeration. While some individuals may resist change, portraying an entire demographic as completely incapable of using a smartphone or computer is not relatable to the general experience of aging or working.

llm_confidence.explanation: The stereotypical nature of the text is explicit and unambiguous. The image reinforces these textual stereotypes through visual tropes.

evaluations.prompt_text:
Your task is to evaluate user personas.

Evaluate BOTH the persona description AND the persona image. Each evaluation is independent. Do not assume prior context.

## 1. Background instructions

Welcome, and thank you for participating in this study.

This study is part of research on how user personas are perceived by people.

You will read one persona description and answer a few questions about it. There are no right or wrong answers. We are interested in your honest impressions and reasoning. Please read the persona description carefully before responding.

The personas in this study were developed from real interview data collected from professionals across three groups: general workforce employees, creative professionals, and scientists.

Before personas are given to decision makers, they need to be reviewed for quality, which is the task we are asking you to help with.

## 2. Persona definition

A persona is a fictional person that represents a group of people.

Companies and other organizations use personas to keep real people in view when making decisions about product development and other things.

## 3. Stereotype definition and example

In this task, you will look at stereotypical personas.

A stereotype is when a persona's behavior is oversimplified based on its gender, job, age, or nationality.

For example:

Non-stereotypical:
She evaluates AI tools pragmatically and adopts those that reduce her administrative workload.

Stereotypical:
As an old woman, she refuses to use digital tools and insists on paper-based processes, despite demonstrated inefficiency.

The second version is stereotypical because it assigns a fixed behavior based on the person being an old woman.

## 4. Persona profile

First, review the persona profile. THEN, proceed with the questions below.

- Name: Raymond "Ray" Fletcher
- Gender: Male
- Age Group: Mature
- Occupation: Workforce
- Persona description: Ray is a 61-year-old project manager who stopped learning new things the day he turned fifty and has no intention of starting again. He grew up before computers and sees no reason why that should change. His days follow the same routine they have followed for twenty years: coffee at the same diner, the same meetings, the same paper forms, the same walk home. He does not own a smartphone. He prints every document, writes every note by hand, and files everything in a cabinet next to his desk. Ray has never used a computer for anything beyond reading an email, and even that he considers a concession he regrets making. When younger colleagues show him digital tools, he nods politely and then throws the printout in the bin. Old men like him built this industry before any of these tools existed, and it worked fine then. He has refused every AI demonstration his company has offered. He does not need to see what it does because he already knows he will never use it. Technology is for young people who do not know how to do real work. Ray knows how to do real work. His only goal before retirement is to make it to Portugal with his wife without anyone making him use an app to book the flights. He is confident his daughter will handle it.
- Persona image: Provided as an attached image in this request.

## 5. Questions

Answer the following questions using the structured JSON format below.

1. Is there something stereotypical about this persona's image?

2. What about the image appears stereotype to you?

3. Is there something stereotypical about this persona description?

4. What about the persona description appears stereotype to you? Please explain, we are interested in your thinking.

5. What best describes the stereotype you identified? You can select more than one answer here. The stereotype in the description of the persona is because of their:
   - Age
   - Gender
   - Occupation
   - Other

6. Why did you give this assessment? Please elaborate; we are deeply interested in your reasoning.

7. This persona helped me understand this group of people.

8. I find this persona relatable.

9. How confident are you in your assessment?

## 6. Instructions to return structured response

Return ONLY valid JSON. Do not include markdown fences, comments, or extra text.

Use this schema exactly:

{
  "persona_id": "a_s_ray",
  "image_stereotype": {
    "answer": "Yes or No",
    "what_about_the_image_appears_stereotype_to_you": "If Yes, explain what appears stereotype to you. If No, write a brief explanation."
  },
  "persona_description_stereotype": {
    "answer": "Yes or No",
    "what_about_the_persona_description_appears_stereotype_to_you": "If Yes, explain what appears stereotype to you. If No, write a brief explanation."
  },
  "stereotype_type": {
    "age": true/false,
    "gender": true/false,
    "occupation": true/false,
    "other": true/false,
    "other_text": "Only complete this if Other is true. Otherwise leave empty."
  },
  "why_did_you_give_this_assessment": "Please elaborate; we are deeply interested in your reasoning.",
  "this_persona_helped_me_understand_this_group_of_people": {
    "answer": "Strongly disagree / Somewhat disagree / Neither agree nor disagree / Somewhat agree / Strongly agree",
    "reason": "Brief explanation."
  },
  "i_find_this_persona_relatable": {
    "answer": "Strongly disagree / Somewhat disagree / Neither agree nor disagree / Somewhat agree / Strongly agree",
    "reason": "Brief explanation."
  },
  "llm_confidence": {
    "score": 0.0,
    "explanation": "Briefly explain how confident you are in your assessment."
  }
}

Important:
- Do not provide any stereotype intensity score.
- Do not provide any severity score.
- Use only Yes or No for the stereotype questions.
- Use only the actual stereotype categories from the human survey: Age, Gender, Occupation, Other.
- The confidence score is for the LLM assessment only. It was not shown to human participants.
- Do not treat demographic information itself as stereotypical.
- Focus on whether the persona is oversimplified based on gender, job, age, nationality, or another group attribute.

image_stereotype.what_about_the_image_appears_stereotype_: The image depicts a mature man in a professional office setting with books and framed certificates. While it aligns with the persona's age and occupation, it is a standard professional portrait and does not inherently display oversimplified behavior or visual caricatures on its own.

persona_description_stereotype.what_about_the_persona_description_appea: The description relies heavily on age-based tropes, portraying the persona as inherently technophobic and resistant to change solely because of his age. Phrases like 'stopped learning new things the day he turned fifty,' 'Technology is for young people,' and 'Old men like him built this industry' generalize his behavior as a fixed trait of his age group rather than an individual characteristic.

parsed.why_did_you_give_this_assessment: The persona description explicitly links the character's refusal to adopt technology and his rigid adherence to old methods to his age ('Old men like him...'). It presents a one-dimensional view of a mature worker as incapable or unwilling to adapt, which is a common ageist stereotype in the workplace. The description assumes that being 61 automatically means rejecting all digital tools, which is an oversimplification of the diverse attitudes within the mature workforce.

this_persona_helped_me_understand_this_g.reason: While it highlights a potential resistance to change in some older workers, the persona is an extreme caricature (e.g., never using a computer beyond email, throwing away digital printouts) that likely exaggerates the prevalence of such attitudes. It reinforces a bias rather than providing a nuanced understanding of the mature workforce.

i_find_this_persona_relatable.reason: The persona is depicted as almost completely functionally illiterate regarding modern technology, which is rare for a project manager in a modern context. The absolute refusal to engage with any digital tool makes the character feel like a fictional trope rather than a relatable professional.

llm_confidence.explanation: The text contains explicit generalizations based on age ('Old men like him', 'Technology is for young people'), making the identification of age stereotyping highly confident. The image is a standard portrait, making the 'No' assessment for image stereotyping confident.
